scenario: Beacon Manufacturer runs a Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot service that runs 24 workers in parallel across a packaging line and dispatches 9000 jobs per hour to assembly stations. The configuration still uses the legacy zeebe.client.worker prefix for the thread pool size and the platform team must move to the documented replacement key in the 8.8 properties reference before the next rollout.
question: Which property listed in the Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot starter reference replaces the legacy zeebe.client.worker.threads key?

options.text: The documented replacement property for the legacy zeebe.client.worker.threads key in Camunda 8.8 is camunda.client.worker.threads under the Spring Boot starter properties reference.
The documented replacement property for the legacy zeebe.client.worker.threads key in Camunda 8.8 is camunda.client.execution-threads under the Spring Boot starter properties reference.
The documented replacement property for the legacy zeebe.client.worker.threads key in Camunda 8.8 is camunda.client.worker.execution-threads under the Spring Boot starter properties reference.
The documented replacement property for the legacy zeebe.client.worker.threads key in Camunda 8.8 is camunda.client.thread-pool-size under the Spring Boot starter properties reference.

a.text: Incorrect. The 8.8 reference moves the thread pool size out of the worker branch and renames it to execution-threads; camunda.client.worker.threads is not in the table.

b.text: Correct. The properties reference maps zeebe.client.worker.threads to camunda.client.execution-threads at the top level of the camunda.client namespace in the 8.8 Spring Boot starter.

c.text: Incorrect. The replacement is hoisted out of the worker subtree; the 8.8 reference does not list camunda.client.worker.execution-threads as an entry.

d.text: Incorrect. The replacement keeps the execution-threads name; there is no camunda.client.thread-pool-size property in the 8.8 Spring Boot starter properties reference.

explanation: Thread pool sizing is a client-wide concern in 8.8 and the Spring Boot starter exposes it as camunda.client.execution-threads, which the properties reference documents as the replacement for zeebe.client.worker.threads.
